title: Mirror symmetry for the trefoil knot
speaker: Emmy Murphy
tags: Homological Mirror Symmetry: Methods and Structures

proof_stages.label: Weinstein and affine handle framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up Weinstein manifolds as exact symplectic manifolds built by handle attachment along contact isotropics, notes that smooth affine/Stein manifolds are Weinstein, and recalls that in dimension four this can be encoded by Legendrian links in boundaries of boundary-connected sums of B^3×S^1.

proof_stages.label: Contact diagrams as a route to wrapped Fukaya categories
proof_stages.summary: The speaker motivates explicit contact handle descriptions by explaining their flexibility, their usefulness for finding Lagrangian cobordisms and exact Lagrangians, and the guiding principle that Legendrian contact homology of the attaching link models the wrapped Fukaya category.

proof_stages.label: Statement and scope of the self-mirror calculation
proof_stages.summary: The central target is the affine hypersurface X={xyz+x+z+1=0}, for which the wrapped Fukaya category is to be identified with the commutative algebra C[x,y,z]/(xyz+x+z+1), giving a self-mirror interpretation, with clarifications about generation, derived categories, and the affine scope of the method.

proof_stages.label: Lefschetz bifibration reduction to matching paths
proof_stages.summary: To pass from polynomial equations to drawable Weinstein data, the speaker introduces contact front projections, Lefschetz fibrations, and then Lefschetz bifibrations in which vanishing cycles of the total space are represented as matching paths in a second Lefschetz fibration on the fiber.

proof_stages.label: Bifibration data for the hypersurface X
proof_stages.summary: For the hypersurface X, a generic linear section gives a D4 fiber, drawn as a three-fold branched cover with a D4 basis of curves α,β,γ,δ and an ordered list of five vanishing paths.

proof_stages.label: Dehn-twist expressions for the vanishing cycles
proof_stages.summary: The five vanishing cycles are rewritten in terms of Dehn twists of the D4 basis curves, using the fact that half-twists in the base lift to Lagrangian Dehn twists on the corresponding spheres.

proof_stages.label: Legendrian handle diagram from the twist words
proof_stages.summary: The Dehn-twist words are translated into a Legendrian link in the contact boundary of the plumbing, with the ordered vanishing cycles recorded by relative front heights in a diagram with four wormholes.

proof_stages.label: Kirby-calculus reduction to the trefoil and LCH computation
proof_stages.summary: Weinstein/Legendrian Kirby calculus cancels handles and simplifies the attaching link to the Legendrian trefoil in S^3, whose Legendrian contact homology is then computed to recover the coordinate algebra and hence the claimed wrapped Fukaya category mirror statement.

examples.name: Cotangent bundle with a Morse function
examples.note: The cotangent bundle equipped with a Hamiltonian of the form one-half norm-squared in the fibers plus a Morse function on the base illustrates how Weinstein handles mirror an ordinary Morse decomposition.
examples.engagement_reason: The cotangent bundle with a Morse-type function is given as the simplest example and its resulting handle decomposition is stated, not computed or analyzed in a specific case.

passes.reason: a standard cotangent-bundle Weinstein model used illustratively for handle decompositions
standard cotangent-bundle Weinstein model from a Morse Hamiltonian, used illustratively
standard cotangent-bundle Weinstein model with an arbitrary Morse function, used illustratively

A standard cotangent-bundle Weinstein model with an ordinary Morse handle decomposition, used illustratively.
standard cotangent-bundle Weinstein model used to illustrate the Morse-handle correspondence
standard illustrative cotangent-bundle Weinstein example, not used to refute a claim

examples.name: Koras-Russell threefold
examples.note: The Koras-Russell threefold is used as an optional application showing that contact-handle methods can prove a variety diffeomorphic to C^3 is actually symplectomorphic to C^3.
examples.engagement_reason: The Koras-Russell threefold is cited with its defining equation and known symplectomorphism theorem, but no calculation or construction is carried out on it.

passes.reason: a non-sporadic exotic affine threefold used to show smooth C^3 topology need not imply algebraic C^3
exotic affine threefold diffeomorphic to C^3 but not algebraically isomorphic to it, serving as a counterexample to smooth type determining algebraic type
exotic affine threefold in a known family, used to show diffeomorphic need not mean algebraically isomorphic to C^3
The Koras-Russell threefold is invoked as an exotic affine variety diffeomorphic and symplectomorphic to C^3 but not algebraically C^3, showing that algebraic equivalence fails.
A nonsporadic exotic affine threefold cited as diffeomorphic but not algebraically isomorphic to C^3.
It is invoked as an exotic affine threefold diffeomorphic but not algebraically isomorphic to C^3, refuting topology determining affine algebraic type.
used as an algebraically exotic affine threefold diffeomorphic but not algebraically isomorphic to C^3
used as an exotic affine threefold diffeomorphic but not algebraically isomorphic to C^3
Cited as an exotic affine threefold diffeomorphic to C^3 but algebraically distinct from it, showing smooth type need not determine algebraic type.
exotic affine threefold used as a positive test case for proving symplectomorphism to C^3, not as a sporadic classification exception
Cited as a diffeomorphic-but-not-algebraically-isomorphic copy of affine 3-space, so it witnesses failure of smooth type to determine algebraic type.
Used as an exotic affine threefold diffeomorphic/symplectomorphic but not algebraically isomorphic to C^3.

examples.name: Exact Lagrangian twisted sphere bundle / Klein bottle
examples.note: A stabilized affine hypersurface containing an exact Lagrangian mapping torus, specializing to a Klein bottle in dimension two, illustrates how contact pictures make closed exact Lagrangians visible by drawing them.
examples.engagement_reason: The exact Lagrangian twisted sphere bundle/Klein bottle is described as existing inside a hypersurface, but the speaker does not construct or verify it in the talk.

passes.reason: an ordinary nonorientable exact Lagrangian mapping torus/Klein-bottle example used as a constructible Lagrangian
nonorientable twisted sphere-bundle exact Lagrangian family, with the Klein bottle as the n=2 case, used as a construction example
nontrivial twisted sphere-bundle/Klein-bottle Lagrangian, used as a construction example rather than to refute a claim
The twisted sphere bundle/Klein bottle is presented as a positive example of a closed exact Lagrangian produced by the construction.
A standard twisted sphere-bundle/Klein-bottle type exact Lagrangian used as a positive construction example.
The twisted sphere mapping torus/Klein bottle is a nonorientable exact Lagrangian family constructed as an example, not a sporadic or extremal object.
serves as a positive example of a closed exact Lagrangian produced by the contact description
illustrates a constructed exact nonorientable Lagrangian mapping torus rather than a failure case

ordinary nontrivial sphere-bundle/Klein-bottle Lagrangian used to illustrate constructing closed exact Lagrangians
Constructed as a positive example of a closed exact Lagrangian, not as an obstruction or sporadic object.

examples.note: In response to a clarification about plumbings in real dimension two, the punctured torus is cited as a simple surface obtained by plumbing two annuli.

examples.name: Local z-squared branch monodromy
examples.note: The map z↦z^2 is invoked only to make concrete why a path around a branch point exchanges two sheets in the D4 branch-cover diagram.
examples.engagement_reason: The local z-squared branch monodromy is used to reason through the branch-cover diagram, explaining how monodromy around a branch point exchanges sheets and changes which critical dots are connected.

passes.reason: the local z↦z^2 branch point collapses two sheets and is used only to explain monodromy
